title: Skills vs. MCP: two ways to get live options data into your AI
author: CameraGlass6957
selftext:
AI tools like Claude and ChatGPT can now pull live market data from external platforms mid-conversation. I built an options analytics platform that surfaces dealer gamma positioning, IV rank, skew, and trade signals, and I've been exploring both ways to connect it to AI. Wanted to share what I've learned and hear how you'd actually use this in your trading setup.

**MCP (Model Context Protocol)** is a direct connection between your AI and a data source. You paste a URL into Claude or ChatGPT (dev mode), authorize it, and you can ask things like "what's the gamma setup on TSLA" or "show me bullish screener candidates" in plain English. No code, no config files. Works in [Claude.ai](http://Claude.ai), ChatGPT, Cursor, and other clients.

**Skills** are instruction files you add to Claude Code or similar terminal-based agents. The AI reads a [SKILL.md](http://SKILL.md) file and hits API endpoints on your behalf using curl and an API key. Setup is pretty light (drop a file into your skills folder or upload a zip), and it gives you more control if you want to build custom workflows around the data.

From what I've seen, MCP is the easier path for most people. One drawback with too many MCPs is that their tool definitions sit in context whether you're using them or not. Skills only load when relevant, which is why some people prefer them for keeping context clean.

For your trading workflow, which would you actually use? Is there a hard no for either one, or are you ok trying both if the product is actually useful?

comments.author: CameraGlass6957
comments.body: If you want to try the MCP approach with options data, I wrote up how to connect it here: [https://www.reddit.com/r/ai\_trading/comments/1s75d0v/built\_an\_mcp\_server\_for\_options\_data\_so\_claude/](https://www.reddit.com/r/ai_trading/comments/1s75d0v/built_an_mcp_server_for_options_data_so_claude/)

comments.author: Responsible_Nerve_52
comments.body: Where is the option data sourced from?

replies.author: CameraGlass6957
replies.body: Raw data comes from polygon, the processing pipeline is custom
